channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump's dismissive joke about US women's Olympic hockey team reveals inconsistencies in his support for women's sports
item.description: Following the United States' victories in both the men's and women's ice hockey events at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan-Cortina, President Donald Trump congratulated the men's team via a locker-ro...

item.title: Green Party by-election victory signals further fragmentation of Britain’s traditional two-party system
item.description: The Green Party’s victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election marks a historic breakthrough and underlines the growing volatility of British politics. Hannah Spencer became the first Green candidate ...

item.title: Labour falls to third place as Greens win Gorton and Denton by-election in major upset
item.description: The Green Party has secured a historic victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election, overturning what had long been considered a safe Labour seat and dealing a significant political blow to Prime Mini...

item.title: Belgorod Hit by Ukrainian Missiles as IMF Approves $8.1bn Aid Package for Ukraine
item.description: Russian authorities reported significant damage in the border town of Belgorod following missile strikes on energy facilities, causing disruptions to electricity, water, and heating. This marks the se...

item.title: The Kākāpō: Conservation Efforts to Save New Zealand’s Flightless Parrot
item.description: The Kākāpō, also known as the Owl Parrot, is one of the most unique birds in the world, native to New Zealand’s remote islands. This nocturnal, flightless parrot is the heaviest on Earth, reaching up ...

item.title: Newcastle United beat Qarabag 3-2 to reach Champions League last 16 with 9-3 aggregate win
item.description: Newcastle United progressed into the UEFA Champions League last 16 after defeating Qarabag 3-2 in the second leg of their knockout play-off at St James’ Park on 24 February 2026, securing a 9-3 aggreg...

item.title: Police Sergeant Dismissed for Faking Remote Work Using Laptop Trick
item.description: An Avon and Somerset Police sergeant, identified only as Sergeant X, has been dismissed and banned from law enforcement after admitting to faking her work-from-home hours using a simple trick. During ...

item.title: How the CJNG Cartel Uses Technology to Strengthen Its Operations
item.description: The Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), one of Mexico's most powerful drug organizations, has become a leader in using modern technologies such as AI, drones, and social media to expand its operatio...

item.title: Senate Report Estimates $20.9 Billion in Identity Theft Losses Linked to Major Data Broker Breaches
item.description: Congressional Democrats on the Joint Economic Committee (JEC) have released a minority report estimating that identity theft tied to four major data broker-related breaches has resulted in more than $...

item.title: Exploring How Men Experience Friendship and Intimacy
item.description: This podcast feature examines the nature of male friendship and questions whether emotional openness is essential for meaningful bonds between men. Josh Halliday, the Guardian’s north of England edito...

item.title: Study finds vegetarian and vegan diets linked to higher bowel cancer risk but lower risk of several other cancers
item.description: A comprehensive study conducted by the University of Oxford has revealed nuanced health outcomes associated with meat-free diets. Analysing data from over 1.8 million participants across the UK, US, T...

item.title: Plaid Cymru and Reform UK emerge as frontrunners ahead of Senedd election, new polling suggests
item.description: Rhun ap Iorwerth has framed May’s Senedd election as a decisive contest between Plaid Cymru and Reform UK, arguing that Labour’s long-standing dominance in Wales is coming to an end. Speaking ahead of...

item.title: Six English clubs await last-16 opponents as Champions League knockout draw shapes route to Budapest final
item.description: The Champions League last-16 draw is set to determine not only the immediate opponents for six English clubs but also their potential path to the final in Budapest. For the first time, Arsenal, Manche...

item.title: Pisces Horoscope for February 27, 2026: Gradual Emotional Relief and Steady Progress
item.description: The horoscope for Pisces on February 27, 2026, suggests a gradual sense of relief as recent challenges begin to subside. Rather than a sudden shift, the day brings a steady emotional recovery supporte...

item.title: Juvenile Dinosaurs Lived Independently, Occupying Distinct Ecological Niches from Adults
item.description: New research from the University of Maryland led by Thomas R. Holtz Jr. reveals that juvenile dinosaurs were largely independent from their parents, unlike most modern mammals. Young dinosaurs, shortl...

item.title: Study Finds AI Models Resort to Nuclear Options in Simulated Wargames Amid Pentagon-Anthropic Tensions
item.description: A recent study by Professor Kenneth Payne at King's College London examined how advanced AI models from Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic behave in simulated wargames involving fictional nuclear-armed sup...

item.title: Hillary Clinton Gives Closed-Door Deposition to House Oversight Committee on Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Hillary Clinton testified under oath in a closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee as part of its ongoing inquiry into Jeffrey Epstein and his network of powerful associates. During...

item.title: Prosecutors drop two charges against Alexander brothers in sex trafficking trial
item.description: Federal prosecutors in Manhattan have decided to drop two charges against the Alexander brothers, accused of drugging, raping, and trafficking numerous women in a decade-long campaign of violence. The...

item.title: US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva overshadowed by sweeping American demands and threat of military action
item.description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran in Geneva have concluded with both sides offering cautiously optimistic but markedly different assessments of progress. A senior US official described t...

item.title: Royal Mail executives to face MPs over delays in letter deliveries
item.description: Royal Mail executives are to be summoned to appear before Parliament following widespread complaints regarding delays in letter delivery. The Business and Trade Committee had initially given the compa...
